{"primary_mode":"indexing","declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"],"rationale":"Switch to AdamW, raise the learning rate slightly, and finally enable the cosine scheduler already present in the script; this targets optimizer dynamics without adding extra architectural risk.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = "adam"
LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4
WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)

# --- LR schedule hyperparameters --------------------------------------------
USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False
WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2
LR_DECAY_FACTOR = 0.1
LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]

# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 64
NUM_WORKERS = 0","new":"OPTIMIZER = "adamw"
LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4
WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4
MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)

# --- LR schedule hyperparameters --------------------------------------------
USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True
WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2
LR_DECAY_FACTOR = 0.1
LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]

# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_WORKERS = 0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"],"rationale":"Add light label smoothing to regularize the classifier without changing the training dynamics or runtime, which is a low-risk next refinement after the optimizer/schedule gain.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["caching","indexing"],"rationale":"Undo the regularization change that hurt validation loss and nudge AdamW to a slightly stronger learning rate, which is the cheapest remaining way to test whether the current run is under-stepping.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"],"rationale":"The current run looks slightly over-regularized; reducing AdamW weight decay is a low-cost way to let the small CNN fit the CIFAR-10 training set a bit more aggressively without changing runtime.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"],"rationale":"The last gain came from reducing weight decay, so a further small relaxation is the cleanest next probe while preserving the rest of the converged setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"],"rationale":"The run regressed when weight decay was lowered too far; a small interpolation back toward the previous best is the cheapest next probe and may recover the sweet spot without changing compute.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The optimizer and regularization are already near a good region; the lowest-risk remaining change is to widen the classifier bottleneck slightly, which can add capacity without meaningfully increasing runtime.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The classifier bottleneck was the last capacity knob that improved the run, so a modest further widening is the safest next architectural probe with minimal runtime impact.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The wider classifier overshot the last good setting; returning to the narrower bottleneck is the most likely way to recover the current best region before trying any new capacity move.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"],"rationale":"The last gains came from tuning weight decay in a narrow band, so a slightly finer step around the current best is the most likely way to squeeze out another small improvement without changing the architecture.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"],"rationale":"The finer weight-decay reduction moved off the best visible point, so restoring the previous setting is the most reliable next move before trying any new search axis.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.4e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
